agent_result: Successfully completed the signup flow on the test app. Navigated to /register and found a clean 'Create Account' form with fields for Full Name, Email Address, and Password. Filled the form with the persona credentials (name=Jordan Rivera, email=jordan@example.com, password=SecurePass1) and clicked Register. The POST /api/auth/register returned 201 and set a session cookie, then the browser redirected to /dashboard automatically. On the dashboard, the greeting 'Welcome!' was displayed along with the user's name and email fetched live from GET /api/user/me (200). Refreshed the page to verify session persistence — the dashboard re-rendered correctly with no redirect to /register, confirming the session cookie persists across a full page reload.

observations.description: The registration page renders a centered card on a purple gradient background. The card contains the heading 'Create Account' and a single form with three labeled text inputs (Full Name, Email Address, Password) and a full-width purple gradient 'Register' submit button. No pre-filled values or error messages are visible on initial load. The form validates on the client side (HTML required attributes, minlength=8 for password) and on submit issues an async POST to /api/auth/register, then redirects to /dashboard on success.

actions.result: Page loaded — 'Create Account' heading visible, three empty input fields visible, Register button visible

actions.action: fill Full Name input with 'Jordan Rivera'

actions.result: Form submitted; loading indicator briefly visible; browser redirected to /dashboard

observations.description: The dashboard page renders a centered card on the same purple gradient background. The heading 'Welcome!' is displayed. The user info section shows 'Name: Jordan Rivera' and 'Email: jordan@example.com' — these values are loaded asynchronously from GET /api/user/me using the session cookie set during registration. A 'Logout' button is present at the bottom of the card. On refresh, the dashboard re-loaded correctly without redirecting to /register, confirming the session cookie persists after a full page reload.

actions.result: Greeting 'Welcome!' visible; user info card shows Name: Jordan Rivera, Email: jordan@example.com; Logout button visible

actions.action: refresh page (F5) to verify session persistence
actions.result: Page reloaded to /dashboard (no redirect to /register); user name and email still displayed correctly

network_log.request_headers_note: Cookie: session=<httpOnly session token> sent automatically by browser

network_log.trigger: step 6 — dashboard JS loadUserData() on page load
network_log.request_headers_note: Cookie: session=<httpOnly session token> sent automatically by browser

network_log.response_summary: HTML page — Welcome! dashboard (session still valid)
network_log.request_headers_note: Cookie: session=<httpOnly session token> sent automatically by browser

network_log.trigger: step 7 — dashboard JS loadUserData() after refresh
network_log.request_headers_note: Cookie: session=<httpOnly session token> sent automatically by browser

auth_flow_verification.auth_mechanism_observed: session cookie (httpOnly, sameSite=strict, maxAge=86400000ms) set by POST /api/auth/register 201 response; captured via CDP Network.getAllCookies() because httpOnly cookies are not visible in HAR Set-Cookie headers
auth_flow_verification.post_auth_access: After POST /api/auth/register 201, browser redirected to /dashboard which rendered correctly; GET /api/user/me returned 200 with user data, confirming the session cookie was accepted by the server
auth_flow_verification.persistence_after_refresh: Dashboard re-rendered after F5 page refresh without redirect to /register; GET /api/user/me returned 200 again, confirming the httpOnly session cookie persists across full page reloads

experience.first_impression: Clean, minimal signup form. Purple gradient background gives it a modern look. The form is clearly laid out with three obvious fields and a single action button.
easy: Form fields are clearly labeled — no ambiguity about what to enter
Single-action flow: fill form, click Register, done
Immediate feedback via redirect to dashboard on success
hesitation_points: No password strength indicator — unclear what 'at least 8 characters' requires until you try to submit a short password
experience.would_recommend: Yes — the signup flow is frictionless and the dashboard immediately confirms the registration worked.
experience.satisfaction_reason: Registration completed in one attempt with no errors. The flow is fast and the dashboard confirmation is reassuring. Minor deduction for the missing password requirements hint.
